zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Tosny於1114年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德時，她的父親Raoul Ralph de Tosny III34歲，母親Adelise of Huntingdon36歲。她於1135年與Walter De Clifford在英國賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1165年8月2日在英國英格蘭賀福爾克利福德享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭奧可福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Tosny于1114年出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德时，她的父亲Raoul Ralph de Tosny III34岁，母亲Adelise of Huntingdon36岁。她于1135年与Walter De Clifford在英国賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1165年8月2日在英国英格兰賀福爾克利福德享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰奧可福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Tosny was born in 1114, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Raoul Ralph de Tosny III, was 34 and her mother, Adelise of Huntingdon, was 36. She married Walter De Clifford in 1135, in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 2 August 1165, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Tosny est née en 1114, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Raoul Ralph de Tosny III, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Adelise of Huntingdon, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Walter De Clifford en 1135, à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 août 1165, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Tosnyが1114年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドで生まれた時，父Raoul Ralph de Tosny IIIは34歳，母Adelise of Huntingdonは36歳であった。1135年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドでWalter De Cliffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1165年8月2日にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードで51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Tosny(이)가 1114년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Raoul Ralph de Tosny III(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelise of Huntingdon(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1135년 영국 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에서 Walter De Clifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1165년 8월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Tosny родилась в 1114 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Raoul Ralph de Tosny III было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Adelise of Huntingdon, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter De Clifford в 1135 г. в местности флэмстид, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 2 августа 1165 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Walter De Clifford en 1135, en Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 2 de agosto de 1165, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
